name: Ribbon Candy
recipeIngredient: 6 - 8 drops peppermint oil or cinnamon oil
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare white cotton work gloves.
2. Butter three shallow baking pans; set aside.
3. In a saucepan, combine sugar, corn syrup, and water.
4. Cook over medium-high heat to boiling, stirring constantly with a wooden spoon until the sugar dissolves (about 5 minutes). Avoid splashing the mixture on the sides of the pan.
5. Carefully clip a candy thermometer to the side of the pan.
6. Continue cooking over medium heat, stirring occasionally, until the thermometer registers 280°F (soft-crack stage), about 20 minutes.
7. Remove from heat and stir in peppermint or cinnamon oil.
8. Pour about two-thirds of the mixture into a buttered pan.
9. Pour half of the remaining candy into a warm small saucepan. Add red coloring to this portion and pour into another buttered pan.
10. Add green coloring to the remaining portion in the 3-quart saucepan. Pour into the remaining buttered pan.
11. Let the candy cool slightly (3-5 minutes for the larger, uncolored portion; 1-2 minutes for the smaller, red and green portions).
12. With gloved hands, lift and fold the edges to the center with a metal spatula until the edges are cool enough not to stick to the spatula.
13. When the red and green candy can be rolled into balls with the spatula, they are ready for shaping.
14. With gloved hands, pull and twist the red candy until light in color, about 2-3 minutes. Shape into six ropes. Place in one of the buttered pans and keep warm in a 200°F oven.
15. Repeat with the green portion.
16. At the same time, have the second person pull the uncolored portion until white. Shape into six ropes.
17. Place the white rope in the center of one of the buttered baking sheets; place one colored rope on each side of the white rope.
18. Press all three ropes together to form a single flat log.
19. Working quickly, have one person stretch one end of the striped candy lengthwise, working toward the other end until the ribbon narrows to about 1 inch wide.
20. Have the second person immediately follow the first person and turn the ribbon onto one edge and fold accordion-style to make the ribbon shape; break off into six-inch lengths.
21. If the candy hardens too much, return to the oven for 3-5 minutes to soften.
Yield: 2 pounds.
